Ukraine’s Drone War Is Reaching Deep Into Russia’s Oil Heartland
headlines.summary: Russia’s Leningrad Oblast lies some 600 kilometers from the closest corner of Ukraine. But on April 15, its governor declared it a “frontline” region. headlines.title: Why U.S. Drillers Can’t Solve the World’s Oil Supply Crisis

headlines.title: Cenovus Warns Oil Sands Growth Is Drying Up as Policy Uncertainty Mounts
headlines.summary: Cenovus Energy (TSX, NYSE: CVE) just posted one of its strongest quarters on record. headlines.title: US Oil Drillers Continue to Add Rigs
headlines.summary: The total number of active drilling rigs for oil and gas in the United States rose this week, according to new data that Baker Hughes published on Friday, bringing the total rig count in the US to 548, down 30 from this same time last year. headlines.title: US Hits Iran-Flagged Oil Tankers While Peace Talks Continue
headlines.summary: U.S. forces struck two empty Iranian-flagged oil tankers in the Gulf of Oman on Friday, escalating pressure on Tehran’s shipping network even as Washington says a potential deal to halt the war could be close. headlines.title: Iran Seizes Tanker Carrying Its Own Oil
headlines.summary: Iran has seized a tanker that appears to have been carrying its own oil, highlighting just how tangled its export system has become under sanctions and blockade. headlines.title: Upstream Oil and Gas Deal Value Plunges Amid Oil Price Uncertainty
